rulings.Case Title: ANDREW STEWART LENNON VS. LELAND HAROLD VAN WINKLE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 7, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFF ANDREW LENNON Petition to Compel Arbitration. Motion is granted. Stipulation is enforceable, there is no evidence that it was signed contrary to defendant's express wishes. The stipulation was entered into approximately nine months ago and the parties jointly worked towards accomplishing the arbitration, which support ratification of the agreement. The stipulation provides that it is not contingent upon advance agreement to the high low sums, although the parties agreed to cooperate in good faith to arrive at an agreement. By its express language, the absence of high low terms and whether they are cost-inclusive in the stipulation is not fatal to the agreement's efficacy. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 7, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT JAMES CHAU MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike is denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al VS. EILEEN BURKE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 7, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANTS JUDY CHAU, WILLIAM CHAU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled, ten days leave to answer. Stipulated facts from prior action do not contradict instant complaint. Defendants lack standing to raise CC section 1714.10. Taking the allegations as true, CCSF states a cause of action for fraudulent transfer and conspiracy. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: YEVGENIY ZINCHIK VS. JOHN DALTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants John Dalton And George C. Hansen'S Motion For Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff Yevgeniy Zinchik January 29, 2010 Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 7, 2010, line 11, Defendants John Dalton and George C. Hansen's Motion for Special Motion to Strike Plaintiff Yevgeniy Zinchik January 29, 2010 Complaint. Motion to strike verified complaint is granted. Defendants demonstrate that complaint is based upon protected activity: the prior unlawful detainer lawsuit that was dismissed, the service of the 3 day notice, and the prosecution of the current unlawful detainer lawsuit. Plaintiff fails to present any evidence that he is likely to prevail on the merits. =(302/CWW)
